session.title: Trust, Accountability and Pace: Fielding AI in High-Stakes Operations
session.participants: executives, university leaders, and senior military officers

notes.text: ai should augment human judgment not replace accountability
notes.clean: AI should augment human judgment, not replace accountability.

notes.text: we bought an ai tool but ppl dont trust outputs so it sits unused
notes.clean: We bought an AI tool, but people do not trust the outputs, so it sits unused.

notes.text: last exercise ai logistics planner cut mission planning time ~80%
notes.clean: In the last exercise, an AI logistics planner cut mission-planning time by about 80 percent.

notes.text: how do we measure trust in an ai system before fielding it
notes.clean: How do we measure trust in an AI system before we field it operationally?

notes.text: decisions that take a human life must have a human who can be held responsible
notes.clean: Decisions that take a human life must always have a human who can be held responsible.

notes.text: procurement so slow the model is two generations old by the time its fielded
notes.clean: Procurement timelines are so long that the model is two generations old by the time it is fielded.

notes.text: uni pilot used llm to triage mental health referrals worked until it confidently misranked a high risk case
notes.clean: A university pilot used an LLM to triage student mental-health referrals; it worked until it confidently mis-ranked a high-risk case.

notes.text: adversarial examples flip a classifier with perturbations invisible to humans
notes.clean: Adversarial examples can flip a classifier's output with perturbations invisible to humans.

notes.text: speed is a value too being slow can cost lives
notes.clean: Speed is a value too; being slow can cost lives.

notes.text: who is liable when an ai recommendation is followed and turns out catastrophically wrong
notes.clean: Who is liable when an AI recommendation is followed and turns out catastrophically wrong?

notes.text: vendors wont disclose training data so we cant assess bias or provenance
notes.clean: Vendors will not disclose training data, so we cannot assess bias or provenance.

notes.text: red team week model passed our benchmark but failed badly on out of distribution inputs from a partner nations data
notes.clean: In a red-team week, the model passed our benchmark but failed badly on out-of-distribution inputs from a partner nation's data.

notes.text: transparency to the operator matters more than transparency to the public for fielded systems
notes.clean: Transparency to the operator matters more than transparency to the public for fielded systems.

notes.text: what is the right reversion plan when the ai is unavailable mid operation
notes.clean: What is the right reversion plan when the AI is unavailable mid-operation?

themes.label: Human Judgment and Accountability
themes.summary: Where responsibility sits when AI advises or acts.

themes.label: Trust, Adoption and Assurance
themes.summary: Why fielded systems are distrusted and how trust might be earned.

themes.label: Operational Tempo and Resilience
themes.summary: Speed as a value, and what happens when the system fails or is unavailable.

themes.label: Procurement and Pace
themes.summary: Acquisition timelines as a constraint on capability.

bridges.rationale: The pull toward speed strains the demand that a human remain accountable for each decision.
A measured 80 percent efficiency gain did not translate into trust or adoption elsewhere.
Both show confident failure modes that erode operator trust.
Opacity about training data feeds the trust deficit that leaves tools unused.
The pace of acquisition lags the pace of capability the same systems can deliver.
Both press on the question of where accountability lands for a lethal outcome.

heuristicHits.name: Principal-Agent Problem
heuristicHits.principle: A delegate acts on their own incentives and private information, not yours, so good delegation is incentive design.
questions: What does the agent gain or lose from each outcome?
What can it do that I cannot observe?
heuristicHits.why: The tool is a delegated agent whose outputs users distrust, so adoption fails unless incentives, accountability, and information asymmetry are addressed.

heuristicHits.name: Base Rates
heuristicHits.principle: Before trusting a vivid specific story, anchor on how often this kind of thing actually happens.
questions: What reference class does this case belong to?
How far should the specific evidence move me?
heuristicHits.why: Measuring trust before deployment is a prediction problem, so anchor on how often comparable systems have proven reliable.

heuristicHits.name: Theory of Constraints
heuristicHits.principle: A system's throughput is set by its single tightest bottleneck; improving anything else is an illusion of progress.
questions: What is the binding constraint here?
Does this change relieve it or just move effort elsewhere?
heuristicHits.why: Procurement, not model capability, is the binding constraint on fielding useful systems.

factChecks.statement: An AI logistics planner cut mission-planning time by about 80 percent.
factChecks.detail: This specific after-action figure cannot be verified from the discussion. Large reductions are plausible for narrowly scoped, well-structured planning, but the number depends on the baseline process and whether humans still reviewed the output.

factChecks.statement: Adversarial perturbations imperceptible to humans can flip a neural classifier's output.
factChecks.detail: This is well established in the machine-learning security literature.

boundaryConditions.text: true, but only when the planning task is repetitive, the data is already structured, and the metric is drafting time rather than end-to-end approved mission planning
true for many neural classifiers, but adversarial training and input validation reduce the exposure, and robustness varies by architecture and threat model

generalisations.principle: AI delivers its biggest gains by accelerating staff work and option generation, while humans retain responsibility for validation, trade-offs, and approval.
Confident failure on out-of-distribution inputs is the central assurance problem; benchmark performance is necessary but not sufficient for trust.
Acquisition pace, not model capability, is often the binding constraint on operational impact.

frames.name: AI as staff officer
frames.gist: The system drafts and advises at speed; a human commander still decides and owns the outcome.

frames.name: Principal-agent governance
frames.gist: Fielding AI is a delegation problem: aligning incentives, observability, and accountability between the operator and an opaque agent.
